candidates.name: Mike DeWine
candidates.notes: DeWine is a traditional Republican Catholic who signs pro-life legislation but won't fight culture wars aggressively. His 2023 failure to defend the Heartbeat Bill against Issue 1 is his biggest weakness from a Christian conservative perspective. His vetoes of trans bills (overridden by legislature) show he is more establishment than populist. NRA F grade as senator is a permanent mark despite improvement to C+ as AG. Term-limited; leaving office Jan 2027.

profile.education: B.S. Education, Miami University (1969); J.D., Ohio Northern University College of Law (1972)
profile.background: Attorney; held numerous offices including county prosecutor, state senator, U.S. Representative, U.S. Senator, and Ohio Attorney General; became 70th governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Bernie Moreno
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 94% — one of the most conservative freshmen senators. Defeated Sherrod Brown in 2024 with heavy Trump support. Key vulnerability is 'cafeteria Catholic' criticism from Catholic Vote and 2019 gun control flip-flop. Colombian immigrant background makes him unusual in MAGA movement. Business background (luxury auto dealers, blockchain) gives him a wealthy donor class profile. Wife Bridget. Sworn in January 3, 2025.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (self-described; criticized as 'cafeteria Catholic' by Catholic Vote)
profile.birthplace: Bogota, Colombia (immigrated to US as child; raised in Florida)
profile.education: B.S. Business Administration, University of Michigan (1989)
profile.background: Auto dealership mogul and blockchain technology entrepreneur. Immigrated from Colombia at age 5. Founded Collection Auto Group. Defeated incumbent Sherrod Brown in 2024.
profile.office: 284 Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 200 North High St, Room 618, Bricker Federal Building
801 W Superior Ave, Suite 1400, Carl B. Stokes U.S. Courthouse
550 Main St, Suite 10-273, John W. Peck Federal Building

aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-opensecrets.excerpt: Non-partisan campaign-finance database. Search the candidate's name to see every FEC-reported donor, including pro-Israel lobby contributions.

aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

candidates.name: Jon Husted
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 71% is lower than Moreno's 94%, suggesting Husted is more of an establishment Republican than a MAGA warrior. His NRA A+ is his signature credential. Appointed Jan 17, 2025 by DeWine to fill Vance's seat. Must run in 2026 special election — reportedly Sherrod Brown may challenge. Former Secretary of State experience gives him unique election integrity credentials. Had been planning gubernatorial run before Senate appointment. Committees: HELP, Small Business, Environment, Aging.

profile.education: B.A. Broadcasting, University of Dayton; M.B.A., University of Dayton
profile.background: Former Ohio Lieutenant Governor and Secretary of State. Former Speaker of the Ohio House. All-American football player at University of Dayton. Appointed to fill JD Vance's seat in 2025.
profile.office: 304 Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: John W. Bricker Federal Building, 200 North High Street, Suite 600
